lessons.subject: Creative Arts
lessons.title: Grade 7 Dance — Core teaching
learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to dance.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body:
In this lesson learners study dance using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to dance. Key vocabulary: Dance, CREATIVE, GRADES, CONTENTS. Describe the design choices, materials and audience purpose before evaluating the example. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
CREATIVE ARTS GRADES 7-9
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
seCtion 1: introduCtion to tHe CurriCulum and assessment PoliCy statement .....................3
1.1 Background .................................................... Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation:
In this lesson learners study dance using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to dance. Key vocabulary: Dance, CREATIVE, GRADES, CONTENTS. Describe the design choices, materials and audience purpose before evaluating the example. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
CREATIVE ARTS GRADES 7-9
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
seCtion 1: introduCtion to tHe CurriCulum and assessment PoliCy statement .....................3
1.1 Background .................................................... Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about dance. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on dance.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on dance?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on dance?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.C: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of dance?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Ignore feedback from the teacher
options.D: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for dance and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about dance and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect dance to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for dance. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about dance without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.

Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on dance.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on dance?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on dance?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of dance?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for dance and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about dance and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect dance to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for dance. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about dance without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about music. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on music.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on music?

options.A: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.B: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on music?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of music?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for music and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about music and teach it to a partner using the step by step approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.1.3 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect music to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for music. Use the step by step variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about music without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.

worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about drama. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on drama.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.

Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on music?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on music?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of music?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for music and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about music and teach it to a partner using the story approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.2.3 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect music to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for music. Use the story variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about music without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about visual arts. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on visual arts.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about visual arts. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on visual arts.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to integrated creative arts project.

lessons.lesson_body: Learners practise integrated creative arts project using concise exam-style prompts and answer checking. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to integrated creative arts project. Key vocabulary: Integrated, creative, arts, project. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about integrated creative arts project. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on integrated creative arts project.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on integrated creative arts project?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on reflection?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on reflection?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of reflection?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for reflection and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about reflection and teach it to a partner using the story approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.4.3 and explain why your marking memo is correct.

lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for reflection. Use the story variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about reflection without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.

In this lesson learners study assessment using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to assessment. Key vocabulary: Assessment, MTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMT, MTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMT, MTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMTMT. worked_examples.question: A learner mixes warm and cool colours for a poster about assessment. Why does the design create contrast?
step_by_step_solution: Name the warm colours used.
Name the cool colours used.
Explain how opposite colour groups create contrast.
Link the contrast to the poster message.
worked_examples.answer: Warm and cool colours sit opposite on the colour wheel, so the title stands out clearly.

worked_examples.question: Describe one improvement to a rhythm pattern used in a class performance on assessment.
step_by_step_solution: Clap the original pattern.
Decide which beat needs emphasis or rest.
Revise the pattern.
Explain how the change helps the audience follow the performance.
worked_examples.answer: Adding a rest on beat three makes the pattern easier to hear and perform together.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on assessment?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on assessment?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of assessment?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for assessment and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about assessment and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.CA.4.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect assessment to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for assessment. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: creative arts.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about assessment without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports creative arts learning at home.
